context: External stabilization session for agents-lab 0.8 readiness. The factory is currently healthy: full local parity passed in the devcontainer, runtime health preflight returns continue, repo bloat/discourse/complexity gates are clean, and git is expected to be clean after this checkpoint. The default scheduler remains intentionally drained: only protected parked P3 external-influence work should remain out of normal local-safe selection unless the operator explicitly chooses that lane. The remaining v0.8.0 readiness blocker is deliberate versioning only: packages are still 0.7.0, so target-version-ready stays false until the operator explicitly chooses the bump/tag/release lane. Recent work hardened runtime health, pi parity, cold capability handling for pi-lens, model-neutral colony policy, optional doctor auth checks, and the /hatch/control-plane-continuity path so report-only read-only intent plans can run without redundant textual confirmation or shelling TUI slash commands.
next_actions: Do not bump/tag/publish v0.8.0 without explicit operator release decision.
Before any long Pi runtime, run pnpm run pi:runtime:health or the equivalent environment_runtime_health_status gate in the current environment.
Keep the remaining planned external-influence tasks parked unless the operator explicitly selects a research lane with --priority p3 --include-protected or equivalent intent.
If continuing local-safe work, add or decompose a bounded task before relying on scheduler:next; the default queue currently has no eligible task.
Prefer local-safe slices that improve packaging, docs contracts, engine boundaries, runtime pressure observability, first-party Pi stack distribution, or worker/intent contracts without dispatch.
